content.verdict: Zawadi — meaning 'gift' in Swahili — is a supremely intimate adults-only boutique hotel of just 10 private pool cottages, set within a former colonial plantation estate on the south Bwejuu coast. The whitewashed coral stone architecture, plantation shade trees, and direct access to a pristine east coast beach create one of Zanzibar's most romantic atmospheres, with none of the corporate-resort anonymity that can blunt larger properties. Perfect for couples who want to genuinely feel cherished and unhurried.
content.best_room: With only 10 cottages, every accommodation at Zawadi is exceptional — each has a private pool, a four-poster king bed, and a large outdoor bathroom screened by tropical vegetation. The Beachfront Cottages are the top tier, positioned closest to the Indian Ocean with unobstructed views from both the bedroom terrace and the private pool. Request Cottage 1 or 2 for the most secluded feel within the beachfront row.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival at the Plantation
itinerary_7_nights.description: Zawadi's driveway runs through ancient plantation trees before opening onto the coral stone estate. Your butler greets you by name, shows you your cottage, and leaves you to discover the private pool filled with tropical blossoms. That evening, dinner is an intimate candlelit affair — the chef's menu changes daily, built around the morning's fish market catch and the resort's kitchen garden.

itinerary_7_nights.title: High Tide Swim & Spa Morning
itinerary_7_nights.description: Check the tide chart your butler leaves on the desk each evening and time your morning around high tide for the best beach swimming. The Zawadi Spa offers a signature 'Clove & Coconut' couples' massage that is as good as anything in the Indian Ocean region. Spend the afternoon drifting between your private pool and a sunlounger on the soft white sand.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Cooking Class & Swahili Dinner
itinerary_7_nights.description: Zawadi's chef leads a morning Swahili cooking class in the plantation kitchen — learning to prepare pilau rice, fish curry, and Zanzibari coconut chapati. In the evening, the fruits of your labour become your private dinner served on the cottage terrace under the stars, with flickering lanterns and the sound of the ocean below.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sunrise Dhow & Seaweed Farm Walk
itinerary_7_nights.description: An early-morning traditional dhow sail along the Bwejuu coastline catches the light perfectly. Ask to anchor near the local seaweed farming area, where women work the reef at low tide — a beautiful and unhurried glimpse of local life. Back at the resort, a fruit platter and fresh juice await on your terrace.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Snorkeling & Sundown Ritual
itinerary_7_nights.description: Zawadi can arrange a snorkel excursion to one of several reef sites accessible by speedboat within 20 minutes — turtle encounters are relatively common on this stretch of coast. Return for a private sunset ceremony organised by your butler: your choice of location on the estate, Champagne, and a hand-picked bouquet from the resort gardens.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Stone Town Exploration
itinerary_7_nights.description: A private car and guide for the day takes you north to Stone Town — the Slave Memorial, the Old Fort, the cathedral built on the site of the old slave market, and the fragrant spice bazaars are all within walking distance of each other. A private lunch at the rooftop terrace of one of the old Arab merchant houses rounds out a genuinely moving day.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Slow Last Morning
itinerary_7_nights.description: Breakfast is brought to your cottage at whatever time you choose — fresh mandazi, tropical fruit, cold-pressed juice, and the plantation's own coffee blend. Spend the morning in your private pool without urgency; Zawadi offers late checkout on request for departing honeymooners when rooms are available.

caveats: The east coast tide system means swimming is only possible during certain hours of the day; at low tide the reef flat is exposed. Your butler will provide daily tide charts to plan around this.
With only 10 cottages and a single restaurant, dining variety is limited — the kitchen is excellent but it is the only option on site, and excursions to other restaurants require a car.
Zawadi is boutique and excellent but the spa is smaller than those at Baraza or The Residence — the treatments are superb but the facilities are more limited.

faqs.question: Is Zawadi Hotel good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Zawadi is one of Zanzibar's best-kept honeymoon secrets — 10 private pool cottages, adults-only exclusivity, and genuinely personal service from a small team that learns your preferences within the first few hours. The colonial plantation setting is unlike any other resort on the island, and the care with which meals and experiences are personalised makes couples feel genuinely celebrated.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: June to October offers the most reliable weather on the east coast — dry, warm, and breezy. December through February is equally excellent. Avoid April and May when sustained rain is likely. The east coast is slightly more wind-affected than the north in certain seasons, bringing kite-surfers to the area — something to enjoy watching or avoid, depending on your preference.

faqs.question: What should we book in advance?
faqs.answer: Given only 10 cottages, book Zawadi as far ahead as possible — six to twelve months for peak season dates. Pre-arrange your private sunset ceremony and cooking class at the time of booking, and inform the team of your honeymoon so the butler can plan special touches from the first day.

faqs.question: Is it adults-only?
faqs.answer: Yes, Zawadi Hotel is strictly adults-only. This is one of its defining qualities and the primary reason the atmosphere is so consistently romantic and unhurried. The entire property — beach, pool, restaurant — is exclusively for adult guests.

faqs.question: What's the best room type for a honeymoon?
faqs.answer: A Beachfront Cottage is the ideal choice — private pool directly above the shoreline, the best sunrise views, and the most immediate access to the Indian Ocean. The Garden Cottages are equally beautiful in terms of interior finish and private pool but sit slightly further from the beach within the plantation gardens.

faqs.question: How do we get there?
faqs.answer: Fly to Zanzibar International Airport (ZNZ). Zawadi is on the southeast coast near Bwejuu, approximately one hour south of the airport by private transfer. The hotel arranges transfers and the drive down the east coast is a scenic introduction to rural Zanzibar.
